description: Mesh-domain algorithms for OCCTSwift: quadric-error-metric (QEM) decimation reporting achieved Hausdorff error, plus planar cross-sectioning recovering nested slice contours. Pure-Swift, on OCCTSwift.
rules: Import both modules: `import OCCTSwift` (for the `Mesh` type and `Shape.mesh()`) and `import OCCTSwiftMesh` (for the algorithms).

The `Mesh` value type is declared in `OCCTSwift` and re-exported here; OCCTSwiftMesh adds extension methods on `Mesh` plus the value types each algorithm returns. The `OCCTSwiftMesh` namespace marker only carries `OCCTSwiftMesh.version`.

`mesh.simplified(_:)` with `Mesh.SimplifyOptions` returns `SimplifiedMesh?`. Set EXACTLY ONE of targetTriangleCount (Int in [1, triangleCount]) or targetReduction (Double in [0,1]); optional preserveBoundary, preserveTopology, maxHausdorffDistance (>=0).

`SimplifiedMesh` reports mesh, beforeTriangleCount, afterTriangleCount, hausdorffDistance (achieved deviation, absolute, input mesh units). afterTriangleCount is a soft target; it can overshoot when preserveTopology or the Hausdorff cap stop collapse.

Slice with `mesh.crossSection(plane:minLoopArea:weld:)` taking a `CutPlane(point:normal:)` (normal need not be unit length; or `CutPlane.perpendicular(to:offset:)`). Returns an optional `MeshCrossSection` (`nil` if the plane misses the mesh).

A `MeshCrossSection` carries the plane basis (origin/normal/uAxis/vAxis), contours: [MeshContour] (closed loops), openPaths (where the plane exits a boundary edge), outerContours (depth-0 loops), and worldPoint(_:) lifting a 2D (u,v) point to 3D.

Each `MeshContour` is classified by NESTING, not winding: depth (0 = outer, 1 = hole/inner wall), isHole (== depth % 2 == 1), parent, signedArea (CCW positive, normalized: even depth CCW, odd CW), area, perimeter, bounds, and points in plane (u,v) coords.

Build a whole slicer layer stack with `mesh.crossSections(axis:through:spacing:margin:)`; `spacing` must be > 0, `margin` defaults to spacing/2 to inset the first/last slice off the end caps, and sections that miss the mesh are skipped.
